resources.string: Small micro-boluses based on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning.
Help for potential adjustments of profile (ISF, carb ratio, and basal rates)
If enabled, Autotune will automatically update and switch to input profile after calculation from an automation rule.
Enable only if you have reliably entered all carbs eaten, with this option sudden rises seen by Autotune will be used to recommend changes to the basal rate.
Enable only if you use free peak. This option will tune peak and DIA durations
Apply average result in circadian IC/ISF
Autotune will not tune circadian variations, this option only apply the average tuning of IC and ISF to your circadian input profile
Include more log information for debugging
Switch on only if requested by dev to send more log information to help debugging Autotune plugin
Default number of days of data to be processed by Autotune (up to 30)
Selected profile has %1$d IC values. Autotune will use %2$.2f g/U
Selected profile has %1$d ISF values. Autotune will use %2$.1f %3$s/U
Error in input data, try to run again autotune or reduce the number of days
Error in input data, increase the number of days
Autotune calculation started, please be patient
Check the results carefully before using it!
Create a new local profile from this Autotune Profile?
Enable TDD based sensitivity ratio for basal and glucose target modification
Uses the last 24h TDD/7D TDD to calculate sensitivity ratio used for increasing or decreasing basal rate, and also adjust glucose target if these options are enabled.
Adjustment factor for DynamicISF. Set more than 100% for more aggressive correction doses.
AutoISF allows to adapt the insulin sensitivity factor (ISF) in various scenarios of glucose behaviour
Enable ISF adaptation by glucose behaviour
Defines how strong ISF can become. Default 1.0 shuts off this effect.
Defines how weak ISF can become. Default 1.0 shuts off this effect.
Weight applied during longer lasting plateaus.
Weight applied while glucose is below target.
Weight applied while glucose is above target.
Weight applied while glucose is rising.
How frequently SMBs will be given in min
Use Super Micro Boluses instead of temp basal for faster action
Enable SMB always independently to COB, temp targets or boluses.
Enable SMB for 6h after carbs, even with 0 COB.
Enable SMB when there is COB active.
Enable SMB when there is temp target active.
Enable SMB with high temp targets
Enable SMB when there is high temp target active.
Max minutes of basal to limit SMB to
Options to increase the actual SMB size which can be delivered.
variable SMB delivery ratio, lower end
variable SMB delivery ratio, upper end
variable SMB delivery ratio, mapped glucose range
Specifies what share of total insulin required can be delivered as SMB.
Lower end of a linearly increasing ratio.
Upper end of a linearly increasing ratio.
Linearly increasing SMB delivery ratio mapped to glucose range.
Factor by which you can exceed regular 120 maxSMB/maxUAM minutes.
A bolus was delivered within the last 3 minutes, skipping SMB
Enable SMB only if Target is even
Enabled if active target is even number, disabled otherwise.
Percentage of maxIOB above which SMBs are disabled.
Max minutes of basal to limit SMB to for UAM
Minimum grams of carbs to display a carbs suggestion alert.
Default carb absorption impact per 5 minutes.
Controls how long bolus snooze stays active after a meal bolus.
Allow autosens to adjust BG targets.
Limits basals to be 3x biggest basal rate.
Basal cannot be higher than 4x current basal.
Attention! Read documentation before changing these values.
Max U/h a Temp Basal can be set to
Maximum basal IOB OpenAPS can deliver [U]
Maximum total IOB OpenAPS can\'t go over [U]
OpenAPS will not add more insulin if current IOB is greater than this value.
BG level below which low glucose suspend occurs
BG value below which insulin is suspended.
When resistance is detected, lower target glucose.
When sensitivity is detected, raise target glucose.
Limiting IOB to %1$.1f U because of %2$s
Increasing max basal value because setting is lower than profile.
Most recent algorithm for advanced users
Most recent algorithm for advanced users with dynamic/automatic ISF
Most recent algorithm for advanced users with AutoISF extensions
Activate or deactivate the implementation triggering the loop.
NO APS SELECTED OR PROVIDED RESULT
Always use short average delta instead of simple delta
Useful when data from unfiltered sources like xDrip+ gets noisy.
Fallback to SMB. Not enough TDD data.
Fallback to profile sensitivity. Not enough data. Reason: %1$s
Open Loop will popup new change request only if change is bigger than this value in %. Default value is 20%
Max SMB value when BG is below 120
Max SMB value when BG is above 120
Increasing this value will increase the speed to send SMB when BG > 120
DynISF factor to increase or decrease the number of SMB
Increase this value above 100 if you want to receive more SMB.
Enable basal from profile when BG > 80 and delta is not negative.
Weight in kg to calculate basal
Average carbohydrates per meal. Estimated value, not exact
Number of days to train the model
Number of epochs to train the data
Number of epochs to train the data
Learning rate to train the data
Learning rate to train the data
Number of iterations to stop the loop
Number of times it will try before stopping the loop if it cannot get a satisfying result
Enable the possibility to delete one day in ML Training
Enable Learning Rate variable for better adjustment
DynISF Full Closed Loop via automatic detection
% of insulin required for SMB in FCL Mode
Child Mode (insulin needs lower than adult)
Percentage of reactivity in the morning from midnight to 11:00
Percentage of reactivity in the afternoon from 12:00 to 18:00
Percentage of plugin reactivity from 18:00 to midnight
Number of AIMI plugin/Loop table lines
Number of AIMI analysis table lines in plugin and Loop tabs
Enable Auto Drive – automatic meal detection – EXPERIMENTAL
Enable Auto Drive V3 – Continuous MPC Engine (DANGER - EXPERIMENTAL)
Autodrive will send a pre-bolus according to the rise detection conditions
Below this ISF value, the condition to receive the pre-bolus is true
Above this value, the condition to receive the pre-bolus is true
Below this target, the condition is true to receive the autodrive pre-bolus
Above this BG value, the condition is true to receive the autodrive pre-bolus
Minimum BG deviation for pre-bolus delivery
Above this value, the condition is true to receive the autodrive pre-bolus
Minimum BG acceleration for pre-bolus delivery
BG acceleration: if it is above this value, the condition for autodrive pre-bolus is true
Used for early meal detection: BG > 100, predicted BG > 180. A small SMB is sent to avoid negative IOB.
Fixed basal 30 min if meal detected (U/h)
When an early meal is detected, AIMI runs a fixed basal for 30 minutes. Enter hourly value.
Autodrive MPC: insulin per kg (5 min)
Upper bound for each 5-minute MPC dose search: this value × AIMI body weight (kg).
Fixed basal 30 min at meal start (U/h)
Manual modes fixed TBR: after pre-bolus 1, set a fixed TBR for 30 minutes
If you set a note with "meal", you receive this value once as pre-bolus.
If you set a note with "snack", you receive this pre-bolus once.
% of insulin required by AI for Meal Mode
% of insulin required by AI for High Carb Mode
% of insulin required by AI for Snack Mode
% of insulin required by AI for Sleep Mode
% of insulin required for one SMB
Lunch Mode Reactivity is % of insulin required for one SMB
Dinner Mode Reactivity % of insulin required for one SMB
DynISF adjustment factor for High Carb Mode
DynISF adjustment factor for Snack Mode
DynISF adjustment factor for Sleep Mode
Interval between SMB for Generic Meal Mode
Interval between SMB for High Carb Mode
Interval between SMB for Snack Mode
Interval between SMB for Sleep Mode
Enter only the first day of the cycle (dd)
First 5 days of cycle, basal reduction %
Basal reduction % for first days of cycle.
SMB and basal reduction % for ovulation.
Luteal phase, set the % to apply on basal and SMB
Adapts ISF/Basal/SMB for inflammatory cycles (Basal-First Strategy).
Toggle ON during acute pain crisis (Increases Basal, Dampens SMB).
How long the flare mode stays active when toggled.
Maximum temp basal boost during flare (Default 1.3x).
Factor to reduce SMBs during flare to prevent stacking (Default 0.7x).
For continuous COC use or no cycle (stable inflammation).
Detect sustained nocturnal rises for pediatric users.
Age used to enable the monitor by default below adulthood.
Start of the resistance detection window.
End of the resistance detection window.
Amount of additional IOB granted for each 30‑minute block while NGR is active.
Automatically send an SMS with your location and BG if a severe hypo occurs.
Phone number to send the SMS to.
BG below which the SOS will be triggered (mg/dL).
Tap to authorize AAPS for emergency SOS.
Adapts DIA, ISF, EGP based on metabolic hyper-function.
Choose how the algorithm determines your Thyroid status.
Force the module into a specific physical state.
Aggressiveness of the safety gate blocking SMBs.
Adjusts insulin based on sleep, activity, and heart rate patterns.
Use Health Connect for sleep data.
Describe your current situation to help AIMI adapt insulin delivery
No active contexts.\n\nUse quick presets or describe your situation.
High BG drop (%1$.1f mg/dL/h), significant reduction;
CRITICAL drop below 85 (%1$.1f mg/dL/h), BASAL STOP;
Moderate drop below 110 (%1$.1f mg/dL/h), basal reduced 50%;
Rapid rise detected (delta %1$.0f mg/dL), emergency mode applied;
Combined delta very weak (%1$.2f), reduction x0.6;
Combined delta high (%1$.2f), dynamic multiplier applied;
BG stable > 160 and combined delta < 1, reduction x0.8;
Predicted BG (%1$.1f) near target (%2$.1f), reduction x0.5;
Predicted BG (%1$.1f) near target (%2$.1f) but rising detected (Δ=%3$.1f, combined=%4$.1f) → no reduction;
Zero basal duration (%1$d min) exceeded, forcing minimal basal;
Night adjustment (22–23h and 0–5h): DIA increased by 20%
Physical activity detected: DIA reduced by 30%
High HR without activity (stress): DIA increased by 30%
High HR (>130 bpm): DIA reduced by 30%
Pump age (%1$.0f days): DIA increased by %2$.0f%%
Final DIA constrained to [180, 720] min: %1$.0f min
SMB disabled due to high temp target %1$.0f
SMB enabled for 6h after carb entry
SMB enabled: meal mode rising (BG %1$s, Δ=%2$.1f, eventual %3$s)
SMB disabled (no active preference or condition met)
⚠️ BG data insufficient, but meal mode forced.\n
⚠️ BG data insufficient or invalid → fallback\n
Manual meal mode: forced basal %1$s U/h for %2$d min.\n
🛑 LGS (Low Glucose Suspend) override → TBR 0 U/h\n
→ rate adjusted based on BG trend\n
File contains 200 lines or less, deletion not performed.
Last 200 lines deleted. Original file saved as \'%1$s\'.
Data for %1$s removed because TIR1DAYIR is below 85%%.
⚠️ No recent BG — using default settings
BG below target stable without COB
CSV file is missing required columns.
Final model training with optimal hyperparameters on all data…
Predictive model suggests higher SMB, adjustment confirmed
Future BG: %1$s, Expected drop: %2$s, Insulin effect: %3$s, COB impact: %4$.0f, Carbs required: %5$d
AutoDrive early meal/snack detection: Microbolus %.2f U, Combined Delta: %.2f, Prediction: %.2f, Acceleration: %.2f.\n
Microbolus in High Carb Mode %.2f U.
CGM calibrating, in ??? state or high noise level
If system time %1$d is correct, then BG data is old. Last BG data received %2$.0f minutes ago at %3$d
Error: CGM data hasn\'t changed for the last ~45m
Sensitivity ratio set to %.2f based on temp target %.0f;
Temp target set, no autosens adjustment
Target adjustment for high BG: min_BG from %.2f to %.2f;
🛑 Hypo Guard + Safety Margin: minBG=%1$s ≤ Threshold=%2$s (BG=%3$s, pred=%4$s, eventual=%5$s) → SMB=0
🗃️ ML Training: insufficient data, no refinement\n
MPC module: dose=%.2f, Kp=%.3f, corr=%.2f, out=%.2f
🎛️ MPC predictive model: %.2f U (%.0f%%) | PI physiological model: %.2f U (%.0f%%)
📈 DIA: %1$.1f min | REACT◁120 Morning: %2$.1f%% | Day: %3$.1f%% | Evening: %4$.1f%%
Limiting carb impact from %.0f to %.0f mg/dL/5min ( %d g/h )
Carb Impact: %.0f mg/dL per 5min; Impact duration: %.1f h; remaining impact (~2h peak): %.1f mg/dL per 5min
, BG is falling (delta %1$.0f), setting basal to 0.
Waiting %1$.0f min %2$.0f s for next microbolus.
Manual meal modes 0–30m [%1$s] → forced basal %2$.2f U/h (override).\n
Forced basal → %1$.2f U/h (%2$d min). Manual meal mode %3$s\n
Early meal detected → forced TBR %1$.2f U/h for 30m (override).\n
Eventual BG > 110 and hyper → factor adjustment.
Eventual BG > 180 and hyper → basalaimi adjustment.
BG > 180 stable → basal x adjustment factor.
Boost => Hyper above 180 with stable BG
When BG is above this value, plateau logic can boost basal.
If |Δ| ≤ this value at high BG, it\'s considered a plateau.
Required quadratic fit confidence for plateau logic trigger.
Upper basal limit during plateau (× profile basal).
Minimum absolute rate when applying kicker.
Duration of the first intensification stage.
After setting 0 U/h, wait this long before safe micro-resume.
Fraction of basal to resume after 0 U/h (relative to profile basal).
Small multiplier bonus to "get moving" during plateau.
If Δ exceeds this value, reduce/weaken intensification.
Analyzes glycemia metrics and suggests profile adjustments
Hypo risk detected (MaxSMB potentially too aggressive).
Meal management can be improved (Modes or SMB).
Basal share is imbalanced relative to TDD.
This is only a suggestion. No automatic profile update will occur.
This is an automated analysis based on your data.
Optional: Your custom Google Cloud Client ID for account login
Optional: Your custom Google Cloud Client Secret
Prefer account token over manual API key
Enter your Google Gemini API Key (AIza...)
Enter your Anthropic Claude API Key
Use Open Food Facts database (free, no API key required) instead of FatSecret
The Advisor suggests the following changes:\n\n
Note: this is only a suggestion. No automatic profile update will occur.
For remote assistance, enter the expert code.
Below is the diagnostic data for analysis.
Failed to gather data: report is empty.
Operating with base parameters. No anomalies detected.
Configure API key to enable AI Coach.
Good work, but small adjustments possible.
PKPD is disabled while TIR is low.
Reduce DIA (%1$s -> %2$sh) to speed up action.
Move peak earlier (%1$s min) for more reactivity.
Strengthen ISF fusion (Max x2.0) for stronger corrections.
Move peak later (%1$s min) to soften curve.
Increase Damping to reduce late SMBs.
Activate the Second Brain to modulate AIMI decisions
Maximum number of AI audit calls per hour.
Maximum wait time for AI response.
Only apply modulations if AI confidence is above threshold.
Scale basal rates based on neural insights.
🔴 Bio-Sync STRESS (HR %1$d, Steps %2$d) ➝ Peak slowed x1.25
🟢 Bio-Sync FLOW (Steps %1$d, HR %2$d) ➝ Peak faster x%3$.2f
Meal mode rising: IOB %1$.2f > max %2$.2f -> SMB damped to %3$d%%.
Meal mode rising: IOB %1$.2f > max %2$.2f -> basal kept at profile minimum.\n
Google Cloud Project ID (Required for OAuth headers)
